fund.name: Motilal Oswal Large & Mid Cap Fund

allocations.rationale: Equity growth with balanced risk. High 3-year return (26.9%), low expense ratio (1.1%), diversified across large/mid-cap stocks
Hybrid mix for stability. 23.6% 5-year return, 1.0% fee, blends equities and debt for moderate risk
Liquidity for emergencies. Slab-taxed but low risk (0.55% fee), 5.5% 1-year return
Inflation hedge. 58.3% 1-year return, 0.4% fee, simple gold exposure

plan.setup_phase: Step 1: Open a direct mutual fund account on Groww or Kuvera. Step 2: Start SIPs of ₹18,000/month in the above funds. Step 3: Allocate the ₹200,000 existing corpus to the same funds in the stated percentages.
review_checkpoints: After 6 months: Confirm SIPs are active and check for any fund underperformance.
After 1 year: Compare fund returns to their category benchmarks (e.g., Large & Mid Cap vs Nifty 50).
After 5 years: Rebalance if any fund’s allocation deviates by >10% from target.
plan.rebalancing_guidelines: Once a year, adjust allocations if: Any fund’s weight exceeds its target by 10% (e.g., if Large & Mid Cap grows too much, move 5% to debt). Market volatility causes a 20%+ drop in any fund’s value.
plan.rationale: Age & Goals: At 29, you have 26 years to grow wealth. Equities (50% + 30%) will outpace inflation and fund your creative venture in 8 years. Moderate Risk: Aggressive Hybrid (30%) balances growth with stability. Debt (15%) covers emergencies without sacrificing long-term returns. Tax Efficiency: All equity funds are equity-taxed (12.5% LTCG), avoiding slab tax on gains. Gold ETF is slab-taxed but provides inflation protection. Simplicity: 4 funds from 3 AMCs (Motilal Oswal, JM, ICICI, UTI) ensure transparency and avoid complexity.
risks: Market Volatility: Stock markets can drop 20–30% in bad years (e.g., 2022). Your portfolio value will fall temporarily but recover over time.
Debt Risk: Overnight funds are low-risk, but inflation could erode returns.
Gold Volatility: Gold prices can swing widely; avoid over-reliance for long-term growth.
plan.disclaimer: For research/educational purposes only. Not certified financial advice.

sip_step_up.description: Increase SIP by 10% every year to match salary growth

aps.reasoning: The plan has no passive instruments in the equity sleeve. The allocation is 50% to an actively managed large/mid-cap fund, 30% to an aggressive hybrid (which is not purely passive), and 15% to an overnight debt fund. The turnover score is moderate (0.3) due to annual rebalancing and 5-year review, but not frequent. Cost emphasis is high (0.7) as expense ratios are a key selection factor. Research is not emphasized (0.3) since the plan focuses on fund performance and expense ratios rather than deep analysis. Time horizon is long-term (0.8) with a 26-year investment window. Portfolio activeness is low (0.2) as the equity fund has no tracking error or alpha metrics, and the hybrid fund is not passive.

pqs.reasoning: Goal alignment: The plan aligns with the 26-year horizon and moderate risk appetite, targeting equity growth for the creative venture and emergency fund. Diversification: 4 funds across 3 AMCs, but heavy equity (85%) and limited sector/geo diversification. Risk-return: Moderate risk with 11.25% base return, matching the moderate appetite. Internal consistency: Rationale, allocations, and risk warnings are coherent. Tax efficiency: Equity funds are equity-taxed, but gold is slab-taxed despite being a low-risk asset. Some tax optimization could be better.
